channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Bill O'Reilly details Trump's vision for Gaza after speaking with him
item.description: Bill O'Reilly discusses President Trump sharing a video that showcased his proposals for the potential development of the war-torn Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, The Hill

item.title: The number of babies born in South Korea last year rebounded for the first time in nearly a decade
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The number of babies born in South Korea rebounded for the first time in nine years in 2024, welcome news for a country grappling with one of the the world’s most serious demographic crises.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nearly a weeklong closure of Pakistan-Afghanistan border crossing disrupts trade, movement of people
item.description: Officials say a nearly week-long closure of a key crossing on the Afghanistan-Pakistan border has disrupted bilateral trade and the movement of people.
item.dc:creator: Riaz Khan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Morocco urges people to not buy sheep for Eid al-Adha celebrations
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — With Morocco’s sheep herds dwindling at an alarming rate, King Mohammed VI made a rare break with tradition by urging families to forego buying sheep for sacrifice during the upcoming Eid Al-Adha.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ and AKRAM OUBACHIR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian and US diplomats discuss normalizing embassy operations at Istanbul talks
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Russian and U.S. diplomats held talks in Istanbul on Thursday to discuss normalizing the operation of their respective embassies after years expelling each others' diplomats.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The EU beat COVID and ditched Russian energy. Now it must learn to live without US security help
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Over the last five years, European Union countries have been forced to adapt to unprecedented circumstances. They pulled together to purchase tens of millions of vaccines and devised an innovative debt financing scheme to resuscitate their COVID-19-ravaged economies.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK government tentatively backs expansion of Gatwick Airport, country's second-busiest
item.description: The U.K. government has provided provisional approval to the creation of a second runway at Gatwick Airport outside London.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Greek rail disaster report cites errors and major systemic failures
item.description: An independent investigative committee in Greece has released a long-awaited investigation into the country's deadliest train crash.
item.dc:creator: Derek Gatopoulos And Lefteris Pitarakis, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: EU pushes back hard against Trump tariff threats and his caustic comments that bloc is out to get US
item.description: The European Union is insisting that the 27-nation bloc is not out to undermine the United States as U.S. President Donald Trump put it.
item.dc:creator: Raf Casert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 84 Indonesians taken out of scam centers in Myanmar are set to go home
item.description: MAE SOT, Thailand (AP) — Eighty-four Indonesians freed from scam centers in Myanmar arrived at the Thai border city of Mae Sot earlier on Thursday in an ongoing repatriation effort that follows a crackdown by Thailand, Myanmar and China.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fire kills 8 in the Philippines after flames gutted a residential building in less than an hour
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A fire gutted a three-story residential building early Thursday in less than an hour in the Philippines' capital region, killing eight people, officials said.
item.dc:creator: JOEAL CALUPITAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea appears to have sent more troops to Russia to back its war against Ukraine, Seoul says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s spy agency said Thursday that North Korea appears to have sent additional troops to Russia, after its soldiers deployed on the Russian-Ukraine fronts suffered heavy casualties.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Andrew Tate, who faces rape, trafficking charges in Romania, has left for US
item.description: The brothers are also charged with forming a criminal gang to sexually exploit women. Andrew Tate also faces an additional charge of rape.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and ANDREEA ALEXANDRU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 40 Uyghur men detained in Thailand for more than a decade have been deported to China
item.description: The Chinese embassy in Thailand has confirmed that a group of Uyghur men detained in Thailand for more than a decade have been deported to China.
item.dc:creator: Dake Kang And Huizhong Wu, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UK's Starmer will meet with Trump as Europe's leaders worry about drifting US support for Ukraine
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is visiting the White House to try to convince President Donald Trump that peace in Ukraine will endure only if Kyiv and European leaders are at the negotiating table along with Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hamas hands over bodies of 4 hostages to Israel as dozens of Palestinians leave Israeli prison
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas handed over the bodies of four hostages to the Red Cross early Thursday in exchange for Israel's release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, days before the first phase of the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip was to end.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Taiwan condemns China for conducting shooting drills off its coast
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan on Thursday condemned China’s military exercises after Beijing designated an area to conduct shooting drills off the self-governed island’s southwest coast.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israelis bid farewell to Shiri Bibas and her 2 young sons killed in captivity in Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Holding flags, orange balloons and signs saying “forgive us,” tens of thousands of Israelis lined highways as the bodies of a mother and her two young sons, killed in captivity in the Gaza Strip, were taken for burial on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greene threatens criminal referrals at House DOGE hearing
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.), chair of the House Oversight Subcommittee on the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), threatened to make criminal referrals during a hearing on foreign aid on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Iran can manufacture 6 nuclear weapons with newly enriched uranium, UN Atomic agency report says
item.description: A report by the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) cautioned that the Islamic Republic of Iran is producing a large amount of uranium that could be used in six atomic bombs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mystery illnesses claim over 50 lives in Congo: What we know
item.description: Unidentified illnesses in northwestern Congo have killed more than 50 people over the past five weeks, nearly half of them within hours after they felt sick.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio: Ukraine rare earth minerals deal a 'tush push' away
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Wednesday said President Trump is "very close" to making a deal to gain access to Ukraine's critical minerals as he pushes to end the war with Russia, referencing the Philadelphia Eagle's signature "tush push" strategy.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: UN food agency pauses aid to famine-hit Sudan displacement camp of half a million people
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The United Nations’ food agency says it has paused aid distribution in Sudan’s famine-hit Zamzam displacement camp of a half-million people as fighting intensifies between the country’s warring sides, and it warns that thousands could now starve.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romanian far-right presidential frontrunner taken into custody after JD Vance's rebuke of European elections
item.description: Romanian candidate Calin Georgescu is being questioned by prosecutors after Vice President JD Vance criticized a "Soviet-era" shift in European democracies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump envoy Witkoff may travel to Middle East Sunday for Israel-Hamas ceasefire talks
item.description: Steve Witkoff, President Trump’s special envoy for the Middle East, may travel to the region on Sunday if indirect talks between Israel and Hamas on a second phase of a ceasefire deal show progress this week.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Analysts back Trump’s USAID cuts in Africa, say increased trade will better benefit continent's poor
item.description: Some analysts believe that President Donald Trump was right to cut aid to Africa and that increased trade with the continent will fill "the plates of hungry kids at the end of the day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stress and fear roil a tiny, rapidly militarizing Japanese island near Taiwan
item.description: YONAGUNI, Japan (AP) — This tiny island on Japan's western frontier has no chain convenience stores. Nature lovers can dive with hammerhead sharks and watch miniature horses graze on a hill. But the wooded mountain ranges now carry radar sites.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Funeral held for Shiri Bibas and her sons after their remains handed over by Hamas
item.description: A funeral procession for a mother and her two young sons whose remains were handed over by Hamas is being held Wednesday morning in Rishon Lezion, Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Power outage in Chile leaves millions without electricity as officials scramble for answers
item.description: A widespread power outage in the South American nation of Chile left millions of people without electricity Tuesday, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Hamas hostage briefs UN Security Council on the 'pure hell' that was captivity in Gaza
item.description: Former Hamas hostage Noa Argamani spoke to the U.N. Security Council on Tuesday about her time as a captive in Gaza. She also made a desperate plea for the release of the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO nation Poland scrambles air defenses as Russia strikes western Ukraine
item.description: Poland once again launched air defenses after Russia fired long-range strikes across Ukraine, hitting near the shared border with Poland just hours after leaders met in Kyiv to pledge more support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Denver developer shot dead at luxury Belize home in likely 'hit': Police
item.description: Boris Mannsfeld was found shot to death at The Villas at Cocoplum, a luxury residential community on the Caribbean country's southeastern coast, Belizean media outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin appears to contradict Trump on Ukraine peacekeepers
item.description: The Kremlin appeared to contradict President Trump’s claim that Russia would accept peacekeeping troops in Ukraine. “There is a position on this matter that was expressed by Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov,” Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said, according to Politico.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump wants Nobel Peace Prize via Ukraine peace deal
item.description: O'Reilly defended Trump's approach to ending the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which has entered its third year.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Monday but showed slight improvement in laboratory tests and resumed some work, the Vatican said, including calling a parish in Gaza City that he has kept in touch with since the war there began.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Western leaders visit Kyiv and pledge military support against Russia on the war's 3rd anniversary
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Monday marked the bleakest anniversary yet of its war against the Russia invasion, with the country's forces under severe pressure on the battlefield and U.S. President Donald Trump's administration apparently embracing the Kremlin in a reversal of American policy.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Surviving Hamas: The story of Keith Siegel, an Israeli American hostage
item.description: An Israeli-American released from Hamas captivity weeks ago is doing 'OK,' his wife tells NewsNation's "Morning in America with Hena Doba.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation
